aliases: Islamic Movement of the Freemen of the Levant
description: Ahrar al-Sham was a Sunni Islamist rebel group that fought against the Ba'athist regime of Bashar al-Assad during the Syrian Civil War. Formed in 2011, it grew to become one of the largest opposition factions, with an estimated 20,000 fighters at its peak. The group aimed to establish an Islamic state under Sharia law and was supported by Turkey, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia. It dissolved in January 2025, merging into Syria's new Ministry of Defense.

country_links.evidence_quote: on 6 October 2016 a German court has convicted four German-Lebanese men who supplied the group in Syria of 'supporting a terrorist organization', and, on 30 March 2017, two Syrian refugees who were members of Ahrar al-Sham were placed on trial in Munich , Germany for being members of a terrorist organization.
country_links.context: A German court convicted men for supplying Ahrar al-Sham, and Syrian refugees who were members were tried in Germany.

country_links.evidence_quote: By 2013, the Kuwaiti private fund Popular Commission to Support the Syrian People, managed by Sheikh Ajmi and Sheik Irshad al-Hajri had supported Ahrar with US$400,000, for which Ahrar recorded a public thanks.
country_links.context: A Kuwaiti private fund supported Ahrar al-Sham with US$400,000.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 29 March 2019, the criminal court in Rotterdam , the Netherlands designated Ahrar al-Sham as a terrorist organization.
country_links.context: A Dutch court designated Ahrar al-Sham as a terrorist organization.

country_links.evidence_quote: Saudi Arabia , Qatar and Turkey have been reported to have actively supported Ahrar al-Sham. A statement issued by Ahrar al-Sham thanked Turkey and Qatar for their help.
country_links.context: Qatar has been reported to have actively supported Ahrar al-Sham and was thanked by the group.

country_links.evidence_quote: Saudi Arabia , Qatar and Turkey have been reported to have actively supported Ahrar al-Sham.
country_links.context: Saudi Arabia has been reported to have actively supported Ahrar al-Sham.

country_links.evidence_quote: Several European states have attempted small-level engagements with individual Ahrar al-Sham political officials in Turkey.
country_links.context: Ahrar al-Sham has political officials in Turkey and receives support from Turkey.
